nodes.body_markdown:
Add the repo-level configuration and checkout-time guidance plumbing needed for automated review.

Scope:
- Extend config with review enablement or policy, reviewer guidance path, and any review-loop limits chosen in the design.
- Resolve the optional review guidance file from the checkout similarly to `coding_guidelines_path`.
- Thread resolved values through the relevant call sites without enabling review behavior yet.

Acceptance criteria:
- Config defaults and validation are fully covered by tests.
- Missing optional review guidance files degrade safely to null or disabled behavior with explicit coverage.
- Repos with review disabled behave exactly as before.
nodes.title: Add review config and guidance resolution

nodes.body_markdown:
Implement the dedicated reviewer-agent contract.

Scope:
- Add typed models for reviewer results and findings.
- Add a prompt builder that includes code-review instructions, repo coding guidance, and repo review guidance.
- Add Codex adapter schema validation for the reviewer response shape.

Acceptance criteria:
- The reviewer output supports both a clean pass and actionable findings with file-aware context.
- Invalid reviewer payloads fail deterministically in adapter tests.
- No PR-producing flow consumes the reviewer yet.
nodes.title: Add reviewer prompt and structured findings contract

nodes.body_markdown:
Produce the design doc for optional automated code review support.

Scope:
- Define when automated review runs relative to author turns, required tests, and PR creation.
- Define repo config for opt-in, reviewer guidance, and bounded repair rounds.
- Define the reviewer output schema, blocking semantics, and the author handoff strategy.
- Define failure handling, stop conditions, and operator-visible status messages.

Acceptance criteria:
- A merged design doc in `docs/design` covers the control loop and rollout order.
- Downstream implementation issues do not need to reopen the core architecture questions.
nodes.title: Design optional automated code review flow

nodes.body_markdown:
Integrate the automated reviewer into direct PR-producing flows.

Scope:
- After author changes and local required tests succeed, run the reviewer when repo config enables it.
- When findings are returned, rerun the author flow with reviewer handoff context and repeat up to the configured limit.
- Open the PR only after review passes; otherwise post a clear blocking comment on the source issue.

Acceptance criteria:
- `bugfix` and `small_job` preserve current behavior when review is disabled.
- Review-enabled direct flows cover pass, repair, and max-round blocking paths in tests.
- Existing regression-test enforcement and required-tests repair behavior still hold.
nodes.title: Run automated review loops for bugfix and small-job intake

nodes.body_markdown:
Implement the author-side context builder for automated review follow-up.

Scope:
- Convert reviewer findings into compact, ordered follow-up context for the next author turn.
- Preserve file references, round metadata, and truncation rules deterministically.
- Reuse the existing pre-PR repair style so author retry turns stay bounded and testable.

Acceptance criteria:
- The same reviewer payload always yields the same author handoff text.
- Long finding sets are truncated in a deterministic, test-covered way.
- Author retry turns can distinguish reviewer follow-up from other pre-PR repair contexts.
nodes.title: Build deterministic author handoff from reviewer findings

nodes.body_markdown:
Extend the same review gate to implementation PRs created from merged design docs.

Scope:
- Reuse the shared review loop for `_process_implementation_candidate`.
- Preserve design-doc context alongside reviewer follow-up context.
- Keep post-PR human feedback behavior unchanged once the PR is opened.

Acceptance criteria:
- Implementation-flow PRs can run the optional reviewer gate without regressing design-doc handoff.
- Review reruns and blocking outcomes are covered by orchestration tests.
- Existing post-PR feedback loop behavior remains unchanged after PR creation.
nodes.title: Apply automated review to implementation-from-design flow

nodes.body_markdown:
Finish the operator-facing surfaces for the new behavior.

Scope:
- Add deterministic source-issue or PR messaging for review-passed, review-blocked, and review-skipped outcomes as defined by the design.
- Update `README.md` and `mergexo.toml.example` with the new config keys and guidance-file expectations.
- Fill any small logging or observability gaps needed to make review rounds and outcomes legible.

Acceptance criteria:
- Maintainers can tell when automated review ran and why it blocked when it fails.
- The shipped docs match the implemented config surface and control flow.
- Tests cover any new status-comment or metadata behavior introduced here.
nodes.title: Document and surface automated review outcomes
